title: Basic Play Toy Workbench
summary: A simple kid-sized toy workbench from one 1x12 and two 1x3 boards — flat-pack simple, screwed together in an afternoon.
description:
There's a neat trick at the center of this build: laying 1x3 strips flat on the table first, then setting the 1x12 tabletop on top of them before attaching the sides, automatically raises the tabletop 3/4" off the base without measuring a single spacer. It's a small thing, but it's the kind of shop trick worth remembering for other builds where you need a consistent small reveal.

Everything else is straight cuts and countersunk screws — no curves, no joinery beyond butt joints. This is a good first build for a kid old enough to help hold parts and drive a few screws under supervision, since there's nothing on it that requires much force or precision to get right.

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut one tabletop at 18" and one back at 19-1/2" from the Pine. Cut four sides (elevation strips) at 11-1/2", three legs at 19-1/2" and four bottom front and back at 18-1/4" from the Pine. Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to one 4 ft Pine and five 4 ft Pine. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw — several parts here are within half an inch of each other and are easy to mix up later.

steps.title: Elevate and attach the tabletop sides
steps.body: Lay two of the 1x3 strips flat on your work table, then lay the 1x12 tabletop on top of them — this raises the top 3/4" automatically, with no separate spacer to measure. Predrill pilot holes and screw the side strips to the tabletop with 2" screws and glue (or 1-1/4" pocket hole screws if you'd rather use pocket holes).

steps.title: Attach the front
steps.body: Predrill and screw the front piece on with 2" screws and glue.

steps.title: Attach the back
steps.body: Attach the back to the top with 2" screws and glue.

steps.title: Attach the legs
steps.body: Predrill two holes along the top edge of each leg, then attach to the tabletop assembly with 1-1/4" screws and glue.

steps.title: Attach the bottom front and back
steps.body: Attach the bottom front and back pieces between the legs with 1-1/4" screws and glue, tying the legs together for stability.

steps.title: Fill, sand, and finish
steps.body: Fill every screw hole with wood filler and let it dry completely — you may need a second coat to fully fill some holes. Sand with 120-grit sandpaper, then vacuum and wipe down with a damp cloth before priming and painting.

images.alt: Basic play toy workbench for kids
